finding.contract_claim: document.getElementById("root") returns HTMLElement | null, so the non-null assertion `document.getElementById("root")!` passed to ReactDOM.createRoot will raise at runtime if the element is missing.
finding.observed_behavior: The code asserts non-null with `!` instead of a runtime check; the accompanying comment states the element is known to exist in index.html (standard React 18 bootstrapping pattern).
finding.triage_reasoning: The non-null assertion `!` is the canonical React 18 bootstrapping idiom; the accompanying comment on lines 5–6 explicitly acknowledges the null type and explains why the assertion is safe ('we know the element exists in index.html'). The element `id="root"` is a hard requirement of any Create-React-App / Vite template — if it were absent the whole app would already be broken. No real latent bug exists; this is intentional, documented, and idiomatic TypeScript. Significance predicate fails.
